name: Fig Newtons
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the shortening and 1 1/2 cups sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Beat in the beaten egg.
3. Mix in the milk and vanilla.
4. In a separate bowl, sift together the salt, flour, and baking powder.
5. Alternately add the dry ingredients and milk to the creamed mixture. Blend well.
6. Roll out the dough to 1/8 inch thickness on a lightly floured surface. Cut into rectangles (about 4-6 pieces).
7. In a saucepan, combine the remaining sugar with 1 cup boiling water. Cook until thickened, about 5 minutes.
8. Add the chopped figs and cook for another minute. Allow to cool.
9. Spread half of the cooled mixture over one half of the dough rectangles.
10. Top with the remaining dough and fig mixture.
11. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 12-15 minutes, or until golden brown.
